name: design-critique
description: Audit a UI across six evaluation lanes — WCAG 2.2 accessibility, Nielsen heuristics, cognitive load, affordances/conventions (HIG, Jakob's Law, Norman), visual hierarchy (Refactoring UI, Gestalt), and color/design-systems — each backed by an itemized reference checklist. Fans the lanes out to parallel sub-agents (fusion-style panel) and produces a prioritized improvement plan with severity-rated findings (P1–P4) and before/after fixes.

interface.shortDescription: Audit a UI across six evaluation lanes (WCAG, Nielsen, cognitive load, affordances/HIG, hierarchy, color) — each with an itemized checklist — and produce a prioritized improvement plan.
interface.longDescription: Audit a UI across six evaluation lanes, each backed by an itemized reference checklist: (A) WCAG 2.2 accessibility — contrast, use-of-color, focus, target size, reflow; (B) Nielsen's usability heuristics; (C) cognitive load — Hick's Law, Von Restorff, Prägnanz; (D) affordances & conventions — Norman, Jakob's Law, Apple HIG (Clarity/Deference/Depth); (E) visual hierarchy — Refactoring UI and Gestalt; (F) color semantics & design systems — Material Design 3 roles, design tokens, product precedent. The lanes can be fanned out to parallel sub-agents (a fusion-style panel) for a full audit, with findings deduplicated and severity-promoted when multiple lanes agree. Checklists carry adversarially-verified WCAG/Nielsen thresholds and an evidence-grading rule so blocker-severity findings rest on verified facts, not opinion. Prescribes specific before/after fixes with severity ratings (P1–P4) and effort estimates, preserves the existing brand/theme, and asks the user to decide on any recommendation that conflicts with intentional brand choices.
defaultPrompt: Critique the design of this project.
